# Motion Engine — Developer Spec
**Evercrafted App No. 12 | Atelier Tier**
*Generated by skill-to-saas pipeline*

---

## 1. What This App Does

Motion Engine converts a static wreath render image into an emotion-driven animated output. The user uploads a completed render (from the Prompt Compiler, Midjourney StyleSync, or any external source), selects a motion profile and type, configures intensity/duration/fps, and the engine generates a live canvas preview plus exportable frame sequence. Optionally, Claude API maps an emotion description to the optimal motion settings, and generates a full motion brief (emotion analysis, shot list, Etsy/social usage guidance).

**Pipeline position:** Blueprint → Render → **Motion** → Output

---

## 2. User Flow

1. User logs in (Supabase Auth) — tier loaded from `profiles.tier`
2. User arrives at `/app/motion-engine`
3. User uploads a render image (PNG/JPG/WebP, min 800×800 recommended)
4. User selects a **Motion Profile** (Whisper / Breeze / Statement)
5. *(Atelier only)* User optionally types an emotion description → Claude API auto-detects and applies the optimal profile + type + intensity
6. User selects **Motion Type** (Sway / Rotation / Pulse)
7. User adjusts intensity, duration, fps, and background via sliders
8. User clicks **Generate Animation** — frame sequence is computed in-browser
9. Live preview plays back on canvas
10. *(Studio+)* User downloads frame sequence (PNG keyframes)
11. *(Atelier)* User downloads GIF proxy export or copies FFmpeg command for MP4
12. *(Atelier only)* User pastes blueprint ID or genome string → Claude generates a full **Motion Brief**
13. Animation config is saved to `motion_configs` table, linked to blueprint if provided

---

---

## 4. AI Integration Details

### 4a. Emotion Auto-Detect

**Required tier:** Atelier
**Route:** `POST /api/motion/emotion-detect`
**Trigger:** User inputs emotion text and clicks "Detect & Map"

**System prompt:**
```
You are Evercrafted's emotion-to-motion mapper. Given an emotion description, output ONLY valid JSON with no preamble, no markdown, no backticks:
{
  "emotion": "single word emotion tag",
  "motionProfile": "whisper|breeze|statement",
  "motionType": "sway|rotation|pulse",
  "intensity": number between 10 and 90,
  "rationale": "one sentence"
}
Rules:
- calm/soft/minimal → whisper (intensity 20–40)
- balanced/romantic/natural → breeze (intensity 45–65)
- dramatic/energetic/bold → statement (intensity 70–90)
- sway = default motion, pulse = soft romantic, rotation = bold/dramatic
```

**Input format:** `{ "emotion": "string" }`
**Output format:** `{ emotion, motionProfile, motionType, intensity, rationale }`
**Max tokens:** 300

---

### 4b. Motion Brief Generator

**Required tier:** Atelier
**Route:** `POST /api/motion/brief`
**Trigger:** User inputs blueprint ID or design description and clicks "Generate Motion Brief"

**System prompt:**
```
You are Evercrafted's Motion Director. Given a wreath design description or blueprint ID, produce a full motion brief. Respond ONLY with valid JSON, no markdown, no backticks:
{
  "emotionMapping": "2-sentence emotion analysis of the design",
  "recommendedProfile": "whisper|breeze|statement",
  "recommendedType": "sway|rotation|pulse",
  "intensity": number,
  "socialUse": "one sentence about best social platform and format",
  "etsyUse": "one sentence about Etsy listing application",
  "shotList": ["3 short shot descriptions for the animation sequence"]
}
```

**Input format:** `{ "design": "string — blueprint ID or free-text description" }`
**Output format:** `{ emotionMapping, recommendedProfile, recommendedType, intensity, socialUse, etsyUse, shotList[] }`
**Max tokens:** 800

---

## 5. Data Model

### Table: `motion_configs`

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `uuid` DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() | Primary key |
| `user_id` | `uuid` REFERENCES auth.users | Owner |
| `blueprint_id` | `uuid` REFERENCES blueprints(id) NULLABLE | Linked blueprint |
| `motion_type` | `text` CHECK IN ('sway','rotation','pulse') | Motion type |
| `motion_profile` | `text` CHECK IN ('whisper','breeze','statement') | Named profile |
| `intensity` | `integer` CHECK 10–90 | Intensity value |
| `duration_seconds` | `integer` DEFAULT 10 | Animation duration |
| `fps` | `integer` DEFAULT 30 | Frames per second |
| `background` | `text` DEFAULT '#F9F7F4' | Background color |
| `render_url` | `text` NULLABLE | Stored render image URL |
| `config_json` | `jsonb` | Full config snapshot |
| `brief_json` | `jsonb` NULLABLE | AI motion brief if generated |
| `created_at` | `timestamptz` DEFAULT now() | Created timestamp |

**RLS policies:**
- SELECT: `auth.uid() = user_id`
- INSERT: `auth.uid() = user_id`
- UPDATE: `auth.uid() = user_id`
- DELETE: `auth.uid() = user_id`

### Table: `profiles` (shared across all apps)

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `uuid` REFERENCES auth.users | Primary key |
| `tier` | `text` DEFAULT 'bloom' | bloom/craft/studio/atelier |
| `stripe_customer_id` | `text` NULLABLE | Stripe customer |
| `stripe_subscription_id` | `text` NULLABLE | Active subscription |
| `updated_at` | `timestamptz` | Last update |

---

## 6. API Routes

| Route | Method | Auth | Tier | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `/api/motion/emotion-detect` | POST | Required | Atelier | Claude emotion → motion profile mapping |
| `/api/motion/brief` | POST | Required | Atelier | Claude motion brief generation |
| `/api/motion/configs` | GET | Required | Studio+ | List user's saved motion configs |
| `/api/motion/configs` | POST | Required | Studio+ | Save a motion config |
| `/api/motion/configs/[id]` | GET | Required | Studio+ | Get single config |
| `/api/motion/configs/[id]` | DELETE | Required | Studio+ | Delete a config |

---

## 7. Component Map

```
/app/motion-engine/
  page.tsx                    ← Main page shell, tier check
  /components/
    UploadZone.tsx             ← Drag-drop render upload, image preview
    MotionProfileSelector.tsx  ← Whisper/Breeze/Statement cards
    EmotionDetect.tsx          ← AI emotion input + result (Atelier gate)
    MotionTypeSelector.tsx     ← Sway/Rotation/Pulse tab buttons
    MotionConfig.tsx           ← Intensity/Duration/FPS/BG sliders
    CanvasPreview.tsx          ← Live animated canvas + status bar
    FrameStrip.tsx             ← Thumbnail scrubber strip
    GenerateButton.tsx         ← Progress bar + generate trigger
    OutputPanel.tsx            ← Export/Config JSON/FFmpeg tabs
    MotionBriefGenerator.tsx   ← AI brief input + output (Atelier gate)
    IntegrationCards.tsx       ← Marketplace/Portal/Blueprint links
    MetricsRow.tsx             ← Frames/FPS/Duration/Output stats
  /hooks/
    useMotionEngine.ts         ← Core state + frame generation logic
    useCanvasRenderer.ts       ← renderFrame(), renderFrameToCtx()
    useMotionAnimation.ts      ← RAF loop, playback controls
  /lib/
    motionProfiles.ts          ← PROFILES constant, motionParams()
    motionFormulas.ts          ← Sway/rotation/pulse math
```

---

## 8. Deployment Checklist

### Supabase
- [ ] Run `supabase/migrations/001_init.sql` (profiles table)
- [ ] Run `supabase/migrations/002_motion_engine.sql` (motion_configs table)
- [ ] Enable Row Level Security on `motion_configs`
- [ ] Apply all four RLS policies
- [ ] Enable Storage bucket `render-uploads` (public read, auth write)
- [ ] Set bucket file size limit: 10MB
- [ ] Test auth flow end-to-end

### Stripe
- [ ] Create products for all four tiers (Bloom/Craft/Studio/Atelier)
- [ ] Add price IDs to `.env.local`
- [ ] Configure webhook: `customer.subscription.updated`, `customer.subscription.deleted`
- [ ] Test tier upgrade: Studio → Atelier (Statement profile, AI features unlock)
- [ ] Verify webhook updates `profiles.tier` correctly

### Vercel
- [ ] Set all env vars (see `.env.local.example`)
- [ ] Confirm `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is server-only (never `NEXT_PUBLIC_`)
- [ ] Deploy to preview — test upload + generation flow
- [ ] Verify `/api/motion/emotion-detect` returns valid JSON (no markdown leakage)
- [ ] Test tier gating: Studio user cannot access Statement profile or AI features
- [ ] Deploy to production

### QA
- [ ] Upload a 1080×1080 PNG — confirm canvas renders correctly
- [ ] Test all 3 motion types × 3 profiles = 9 combinations
- [ ] Confirm frame export downloads PNG keyframes
- [ ] Test AI Emotion Auto-Detect with: "calm", "romantic", "dramatic"
- [ ] Test Motion Brief with a genome string
- [ ] Test locked feature overlays at Studio tier (Statement + AI features locked)
- [ ] Confirm motion config saves to Supabase after generation

---

## 9. Known Limitations & Future Work

**Current limitations:**
- In-browser GIF encoding is not native — current export is PNG keyframe sequence; true GIF requires gif.js or server-side encoding
- MP4 compilation requires server-side FFmpeg; client receives command only
- Frame generation is CPU-bound on the main thread — large frame counts (>600) may cause UI jank; consider Web Workers in v2
- Canvas renders at 400×400 internally; output should be upscaled to 1080×1080 server-side for production exports

**Phase 2 roadmap (per PRD):**
- Assembly animation (stems → wreath build sequence)
- Parallax depth layers (foreground/mid/background separation from blueprint zones)
- Emotional transitions (one emotion state morphing to another across the animation)
- Multi-scene storytelling (3-scene narrative: reveal → detail → context)
- Real-time preview UI (WebGL or Three.js renderer)
- Blueprint zone-aware animation (focal clusters move with higher amplitude than greenery base)
- Server-side FFmpeg pipeline via `/api/motion/compile` route
- True GIF encoding via server-side gifski or ffmpeg palette
